steps.title: Identify the contrast and its purpose
steps.content: The passage contrasts Ambopteryx (a winged dinosaur with feathers) with Yutyrannus (a wingless dinosaur) using “However”—but the contrast isn't about who has feathers. The point is that both had feathers, despite the difference in wings.

steps.title: Locate what binds them
steps.content:
Both had feathers. The passage explicitly establishes this: Ambopteryx is introduced in the discussion of feathered dinosaurs, and Yutyrannus is directly stated to have “likely had feathers.” The “However” passage confirms they differed in having wings, not in having feathers. A cites no predators, B incorrectly labels Ambopteryx as wingless, and C is never suggested.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
